DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN BUI'S MOTION FOR ORDERS FOR COMPELLING PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AT DEPOSITION AND FURTHER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY FROM DEFENDANT MARCOS ALVAREZ CASTENEDA REGARDING SAME. MOTION GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE RECORDS OF MR. CASTENEDA'S CELL PHONE CALLS ON THE DAY OF THE INCIDENT ARE RELEVANT, HOWEVER, DEFENDANTS NEED ONLY PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE IN THEIR POSSESSION, CUSTODY, AND CONTROL. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DECLARATION OF BRENDAN RADICH IS INSUFFICIENT SINCE IT DOES NOT DETAIL HIS EFFORTS TO OBTAIN THE CELL PHONE RECORDS FROM AT&T AND IT APPEARS THAT AT&T MIGHT BE ABLE TO LOCATE RECORDS BEYOND TWO YEARS. ALSO, THERE NEEDS TO BE A DECLARATION FROM MR. CASTENEDA ATTESTING THAT HE HAS SEARCHED FOR AND ATTEMPTED TO LOCATE ALL RECORDS FOR ANY CELL PHONES (PERSONAL OR COMPANY) THAT HE HAD IN HIS POSSESSION ON THE DAY OF THE INCIDENT. DEFENDANTS SHALL PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THEY LOCATE, OR THE DECLARATIONS REFERENCED HEREIN, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 13, 2018. THE REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFF RESERVES THE RIGHT TO FILE A FUTURE MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IF DEFENDANTS DO NOT COMPLY WITH COURT ORDER. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO DAVIDMCDONALD@PRODIGY.NET JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders For Compelling Production Of Documents At Deposition And Further Deposition Testimony From Defendant Marcos Alvarez Casteneda Regarding Same
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jon-Marc Dobrin, esq. (415-509-2633) for Plaintiff John Bui.
Norine Busser, esq. (415-227-2308) for Defendants.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN BUI's Motion For Orders For Compelling Production Of Documents At Deposition And Further Deposition Testimony From Defendant Marcos Alvarez Casteneda Regarding Same

Motion granted in part. The Court finds that the records of Mr. Casteneda's cell phone calls on the day of the incident are relevant, however, Defendants need only produce documents that are in their possession, custody, and control. The Court finds that the declaration of Brendan Radich is insufficient since it does not detail his efforts to obtain the cell phone records from AT&T and it appears that AT&T might be able to locate records beyond two years. Also, there needs to be a declaration from Mr. Casteneda attesting that he has searched for and attempted to locate all records for any cell phones (personal or company) that he had in his possession on the day of the incident. Defendants shall produce any documents they locate, or the declarations referenced herein, no later than February 13, 2018. The requests for sanctions are denied. Plaintiff reserves the right to file a future motion for sanctions if Defendants do not comply with court order. Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
